notes.title: Photosynthesis is the process by which green pl...
notes.raw_content:
Photosynthesis is the process by which green plants convert sunlight, 
water, and carbon dioxide into glucose and oxygen. This process occurs 
mainly in the chloroplasts of plant cells, specifically using a green 
pigment called chlorophyll. The overall chemical equation for 
photosynthesis is: 6CO2 + 6H2O + light energy -> C6H12O6 + 6O2. 
Photosynthesis is essential for life on Earth because it produces 
the oxygen we breathe and forms the base of most food chains.

notes.title: The Solar System consists of the Sun and everyt...
notes.raw_content:
The Solar System consists of the Sun and everything that orbits around 
it, including eight planets, dwarf planets, moons, asteroids, and 
comets. The eight planets in order from the Sun are Mercury, Venus, 
Earth, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, and Neptune. Jupiter is the 
largest planet in the Solar System, while Mercury is the smallest. 
Earth is the only known planet to support life, largely due to its 
distance from the Sun and the presence of liquid water. Saturn is 
famous for its prominent ring system, made mostly of ice and rock 
particles. It takes Earth approximately 365.25 days to complete one 
orbit around the Sun, which is why we have a leap year every four years.

famous for its prominent ring system, made mostly of ice and rock 
particles.

graded_questions.question:
Complete this fact from your notes: "______ is the only known planet to support life, largely due to its 
distance from the Sun and the presence of liquid water."
options: Earth
Neptune
Mercury
Uranus
graded_questions.source_sentence:
Earth is the only known planet to support life, largely due to its 
distance from the Sun and the presence of liquid water.

graded_questions.question:
Complete this fact from your notes: "The eight planets in order from the Sun are ______, Venus, 
Earth, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, and Neptune."
options: Solar System
365.25 days
The Solar System
Mercury
graded_questions.source_sentence:
The eight planets in order from the Sun are Mercury, Venus, 
Earth, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, and Neptune.
